data.title: Reports Claim Anthropic and OpenAI Are Preparing to go Public
data.description:
Multiple media reports claim that artificial intelligence (AI) start-up companies Anthropic and OpenAI are taking steps to hold initial public offerings (IPOs) as early as this year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Times (NYT) was the first to report that Anthropic and OpenAI have taken the early steps toward going public, joining Elon Musk's SpaceX in what could become a blockbuster year for technology IPOs on Wall Street.

According to the reports, Anthropic and OpenAI have each taken the preliminary steps to hold an IPO in coming months. SpaceX, a satellite and commercial space company, is also moving towards an IPO this year in what could be the biggest market debut ever for a U.S. concern.

Massive Valuations for Anthropic and OpenAI

These three companies would rank among the most valuable to ever go public since Saudi Aramco's 2019 debut, which valued the energy giant at $1.7 trillion. Anthropic is currently in funding talks that could value it at $350 billion, while OpenAI carries an estimated worth of $500 billion. SpaceX was most recently valued at $800 billion.

The potential public offerings of these tech giants could create what some are calling an "IPO supercycle" or "monster listing mania," setting up a watershed moment for Silicon Valley and the artificial intelligence sector.

If all three companies proceed with their plans, 2026 could mark a historic year for high-profile tech listings on public markets.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'A Must-Own,' Says Analyst About Shopify Stock
data.description:
Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP) shares have regained momentum over the past year, rebounding after a challenging post-pandemic reset in the e-commerce sector. The stock is up 52% over the past 12 months, supported by improving sentiment around online retail, digital payments, and the company's expanding use of AI-driven commerce tools. Merchant activity has remained resilient, and consumer spending trends have held steady.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What's driving the renewed interest isn't just a recovery in e-commerce demand. Investors are increasingly focused on how technology is changing the buying process itself. AI-powered assistants, automated product discovery, and faster, more personalized checkout experiences are beginning to reshape how consumers shop online. Rather than browsing endlessly, shoppers may soon rely on intelligent agents to guide decisions and even complete purchases on their behalf, a shift that could redefine digital commerce.

That broader theme is central to a recent bullish call from Scotiabank analyst Kevin Krishnaratne, who upgraded Shopify Outperform (i.e., Buy) and raised his price target to $200. (To watch Krishnaratne's track record, click here)

In Krishnaratne's view, Shopify is well positioned to benefit from the rise of what he calls "agentic commerce," or A-Commerce. The analyst describes A-Commerce as a new layer of retail, where AI agents actively assist consumers throughout the shopping journey. In his words, "Agentic Commerce has arrived, and Shopify is ready to capitalize on an opportunity that has the potential to reshape the retail industry, similar to how e-commerce fundamentally changed the structure of traditional commerce." Importantly, the analyst does not see this as simply shifting sales from one channel to another. Instead, he argues that A-Commerce could expand overall retail spending, much like the original transition from physical stores to online platforms.

From a numbers perspective, Krishnaratne outlines scenarios in which Shopify's gross merchandise value could grow meaningfully faster than current expectations. In one case, the analyst sees GMV growth running about 300 basis points above consensus forecasts for 2026, with upside if adoption of agentic commerce accelerates. While he acknowledges outcomes will likely land somewhere between conservative and aggressive assumptions, the message is that Shopify's scale, merchant ecosystem, and improving operating leverage give it a strong advantage.

What does the rest of the Street think? Looking at the consensus breakdown, opinions from other analysts are more spread out. 18 Buys and 13 Holds add up to a Moderate Buy consensus. In addition, the $180.62 average price target indicates ~16% upside potential. (See SHOP stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Fed's Miran Says Deregulation Will 'Boost Competition, Productivity and Potential Growth'
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran called for easing regulatory measures on U.S. companies and reiterated his that the Fed should cut interest rates by 150 bps by the end of the year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I believe that the sweeping deregulation underway in the United States will significantly boost competition, productivity and potential growth, allowing faster economic growth without putting upward pressure on inflation," Miran said on Wednesday in prepared remarks at the Delphi Economic Forum in Athens, Greece.

Deregulation Could Ease Inflation, Support Rate Cuts, Says Miran

Miran pointed to the economic recovery of Greece beginning in 2009 following the financial crisis, saying it was only possible after the country implemented deregulatory policies across several sectors.

He added that he expects shelter inflation, one of the main drivers behind overall inflation in the U.S., to wind down and that deregulation won't contribute to higher inflation. Instead, it could lead to lower prices and allow the Fed to reduce rates.

data.title: Walmart Told to Slow Down Indian Quick Commerce Due to Driver Safety Fears
data.description:
Retail giant Walmart (WMT) has been forced by the Indian government to stop promoting its grocery business as a fast delivery service because of road safety fears.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fast Delivery Danger

The company's Indian business Flipkart, along with online delivery companies Swiggy and Zepto have been ordered to stop describing their grocery businesses as "10-minute" delivery services.

The quick commerce sector, where groceries and electronics can be delivered to a customer's house in mere minutes, has been racing ahead in India, becoming an $11.5 billion market within five years.

In 2025, consumers reportedly spent over $7 billion, which is more than double the previous fiscal year, on quick commerce platforms. It is estimated that the gross order value will surpass $23 billion by 2028.

However, rash driving, road safety issues and low pay are big issues in the sector. According to the Indian press, delivery drivers went on strike at the end of last year protesting against the conditions imposed by some of these quick commerce companies.

They no longer wanted to have the pressure of delivering in 10 minutes, as well as better wages and social security.

Government Orders Change

It prompted India's labor ministry to ask companies during a private meeting last weekend to stop using the 10-minute branding. It was not clear whether companies would be fined or not for non-compliance. The government is also reportedly looking at changes to social security eligibility.

Walmart, which holds a more than 80% stake in Flipkart, has already moved to drop the branding.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Slips on Mixed Inflation Data as SCOTUS Holds Off on Trump Tariff Ruling
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is in the red on Wednesday as investors digest new inflation data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Producer Price Index (PPI) rose by 0.2% month-over-month in November, in line with the consensus estimate but rising from 0.1% in October. PPI grew by 3% on an annual basis, above the estimate of 2.7% and accelerating from 2.7% in October. The index tracks changes in wholesale prices received by producers and is often seen as a leading indicator of consumer inflation. Several components of the PPI feed into the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Index, which the Fed uses as its key inflation gauge.

Core PPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, remained unchanged from the prior month with a year-over-year rise of 3%. Analysts were expecting 0.2% and 2.7%, respectively.

Many had expected the Supreme Court to issue a ruling on the Trump tariff case today, although investors will have to wait longer as the court remained silent during its opinion day. A decision could come as early as next Tuesday, although the court doesn't announce in advance which rulings it will address. The odds of SCOTUS ruling in favor of the tariffs are at a low 34% on prediction platform Polymarket.

Strong consumer spending failed to lift the market, with November retail sales climbing by 0.6% month-over-month, above the estimate of 0.5% and marking the highest monthly growth since July. Online spending also got a boost from the holiday season and Black Friday, with shoppers spending a record $257.8 billion from November 1 to December 31, according to Adobe (ADBE).

The Dow Jones is down by 0.48% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "…No Expectations of Receiving Anything…" Comcast Stock (NASDAQ:CMCSA) Gains as it Tackles Misconduct Allegations
data.description:
Entertainment giant Comcast (CMCSA) did not expect anything out of its recent donation to the Trump White House's ballroom project. It wants that to be perfectly clear, and did just about everything it could short of tattooing it on executives' foreheads in responding to allegations from Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) on the topic. The response was good enough for investors, who sent Comcast shares up modestly in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

When Warren called Comcast on the table, searching for possible quid pro quo arrangements between Comcast and the Trump White House, Comcast made it quite clear there were none. It was a possibility, after all—Comcast was briefly in the running to buy Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), and a little White House support might have helped make such a deal happen—but Comcast was clear: no such thing had happened.

In fact, Comcast called such allegations "…categorically false." Rather, the donation was done as part of Comcast's history of "…promoting the vibrant civic life of the nation's capital as well as a wide variety of Washington institutions." Comcast proceeded to name drop the list of said institutions it had supported in the past, from the Trump / Vance Inaugural Committee to the Biden Inaugural Committee. This made it clear it was out to support Washington as opposed to one particular party that could help make deals go through.

Unexpected Consequences

Meanwhile, Comcast ran into unexpected consequences with its spin-off of Versant (VSNT). The cable channels that it spun off to create Versant are now no longer available on Peacock, reports note. Once Versant left, and took the cable channels with it, the content on networks like Syfy, Oxygen, USA Network and several others left as well.

This represents not only a huge problem for Peacock viewers, who just lost a huge slug of programming, but also for Comcast as a whole. Why there was no licensing arrangement set up beforehand is unclear at best; Peacock easily could have been one of Versant's first and potentially biggest customers. But it also represents a major issue for streaming viewers, who now have one more service in the rotation to work with in an increasingly glutted field.

Is Comcast Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on CMCSA stock based on seven Buys, 12 Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 22.88% loss in its share price over the past year, the average CMCSA price target of $33.34 per share implies 17% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Trump's Tariff Case Won't Receive a Supreme Court Ruling on Wednesday
data.description:
The Supreme Court issued three rulings on Wednesday, but didn't provide a decision on the legality of President Trump's tariffs during its second opinion day of the year. SCOTUS hasn't announced the date for its next opinion day, although it could come as early as next Tuesday, according to Bloomberg.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier this week, Trump warned that the U.S. would be "SCREWED" if the tariffs are overturned and would be subject to hundreds of billions of dollars in tariff refunds. The tariffs have brought in over $130 billion of revenue for the U.S. since they were enacted.

Odds of SCOTUS Upholding Trump's Tariffs Remain Low

The odds of SCOTUS ruling in favor of the tariffs sit at a low 34% on the prediction platform Polymarket, although they have risen by 13% during the past week. Last November, several Supreme Court justices expressed skepticism that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), the legal precedent behind the duties, allows the president to enforce sweeping tariffs. The IEEPA allows the president to enforce tariffs in response to national emergencies.

The Trump administration has already considered other legal alternatives to support the tariffs, such as Section 301 and Section 122 of the Trade Act.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: BK Analyst Ratings Roundup: Bank of New York Mellon Stock Jumps on Post Earnings Updates
data.description:

Let's dive into all of the updated analyst coverage of BK stock below.

  • Five-star KBW analyst David Konrad maintained a Buy rating and raised his price target to $143 from $132, suggesting a 14.69% upside.

Bank of New York Mellon Stock Movement Today

Bank of New York Mellon stock was up 1.09% on Wednesday, extending a 7.05% year-to-date rally. The shares have also increased 49.84% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Blue Owl Shareholders Sue Over BDC Redemption Claims
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against Blue Owl Capital (OWL) on December 3, 2025.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The plaintiffs (investors) in the federal securities class action allege that they bought Blue Owl stock at artificially inflated prices between February 6, 2025 and November 16, 2025. They are now seeking compensation for financial losses incurred upon public revelation of the company's alleged misconduct during that time.

Investors who bought Blue Owl Capital stock during that period can click here to learn about joining the lawsuit.

Blue Owl (NYSE:OWL) is a self-described leading asset manager, which provides businesses with private capital solutions to drive long-term growth.

Blue Owl concentrates on alternative investment solutions, mostly private credit. To that end, Blue Owl has three major product platforms – Credit, GP Strategic Capital, and Real Assets.

According to the complaint, Blue Owl engages in the provision of direct lending, alternative credit, investment grade credit, liquid credit, and other private financing solutions through its Credit platform. As also detailed in the complaint, direct lending is an activity that occurs when non-banking entities make private loans to businesses. 

In all, Blue Owl said it had $295 billion in assets under management as of September 30, 2025.
data.author: David Craik
